last_item: Navigation/IA — the routed view renders inside a `<main className='… overflow-y-auto …'>` (Layout.tsx) that is its own scroll container; with client-side routing only the `<Outlet/>` children swap, so `<main>` is never remounted and its scrollTop carried over between views (scroll down in a long view, navigate, land mid-page with the new view's header off-screen). Surfaced by the iter-17 nav/IA convergence agent (the other 4 agents converged); verified against the code, then promoted to Mode F because it's a genuine defect, not decoration.
last_result: SHIPPED (4 SHIP, zero blockers). Fix in Layout.tsx: a `useRef` on `<main>` + a `useEffect` keyed on `location.pathname` that sets `scrollTop = 0` on each route change — keyed on the PATH so an in-place re-render of the same view (e.g. backup history auto-refreshing while you read it) does NOT yank you to the top; only actual navigation does. Zero added visual UI/control/copy; added `data-testid='main-content'` + a test (scroll to 400 → navigate → assert reset to 0). Panel: UX heuristics (Consistency & Standards fix, no anchor-nav/back-forward contract broken); Sam (keyed-on-path means no yank off data mid-read); Priya (deep-links land at top like a fresh load, filters/selections live in state/URL not scrollTop); restraint critic ('behavior-correctness fix with zero added surface area; the bug is real and routine'). Gates: typecheck OK, 142 tests OK (141 + 1 new), build OK (dist regenerated + staged), go build OK (outside sandbox). stable_streak RESET 2 -> 0 (shipped a real improvement; the convergence clock restarts). LESSON: convergence is provisional — a late discovery pass can still find a real bug; keep each pass honest (high bar filters decoration, not looking), and verify a surfaced item against the code rather than dismissing it because prior passes were clean. Next iteration: run a fresh discovery/convergence pass.
notes: PURE UX-IMPROVEMENT LOOP. Sibling of the COMPLETED scripts/ralph/ shadcn-migration track. Capability parity is sacred (Vitest suite is the contract); layout/flow/copy are free to change. Restraint over churn -- rejecting a change and recording why is a normal outcome. Frontend/UX only, never touch Go. go build is blocked in-sandbox (snap-confine); run with sandbox disabled. See PROMPT.md + UX-PRINCIPLES.md + PERSONAS.md + UI-RULES.md.
north_star: The indiepg panel is the easiest, clearest, safest way for an indie hacker to run their Postgres -- minimal surface, co-located workflows, honest state, no over-design. Stop when the UX has converged (stable_streak >= 3).
